alert_message: License expired
recommended_action: To resume operational mode, contact EMC Support for license renewal. If you have already renewed your license, install it.

alert_message: The system's license will expire in n days
severity: 3 (Error) 2 (Warning) according to time left and limits
recommended_action: Contact EMC Support for license renewal. If you have already renewed your license, install it.

alert_message: PowerFlex OS is using a trial license
recommended_action: Purchase a license and install it.

alert_message: Oscillating failures reported
recommended_action: Check oscillating failures of the component and take action accordingly. If the oscillating failure does not indicate a problem, change the settings of the oscillating failure window to supress this alert

alert_message: There are oscillating network failures. Download network counters for detailed information
recommended_action: Check the oscillating failure report, that can be accessed from one of the management interfaces. Check whether there is a problem with network links, fix, and restart the counters.

alert_message: No valid MDM credentials are configured in PowerFlex OS Gateway
recommended_action: Configure the MDM credentials in the PowerFlex OS Gateway using the FOSGWTool.

alert_message: MDM credentials are not configured in the PowerFlex OS Gateway
recommended_action: Configure MDM credentials on the PowerFlex OS Gateway using theFOSGWTool

alert_message: The MDM user configured in PowerFlex OS Gateway requires a password change
recommended_action: Configure MDM credentials on the PowerFlex OS Gateway using the FOSGWTool

alert_message: System upgrade is in progress
recommended_action: Monitor the upgrade process, and check that it is completed successfully.

alert_message: PowerFlex OS Gateway version is too old
recommended_action: Upgrade the PowerFlex OS Gateway to the same version as the rest of your system.

alert_message: The MDM is not operating in clustered mode
recommended_action: MDM cluster was manually set to SINGLE mode. Confirm that this is an expected operation. Working in SINGLE mode is not recommended. Prepare the cluster modules (if needed), and return to CLUSTER mode.

alert_message: MDM fails over frequently
severity: 5 (Critical) 3 (Error) 2 (Warning) according to disconnect count and hardcoded values (2/3/10)
recommended_action: The MDMs frequently swap ownership. No action required.

alert_message: Forward rebuild cannot proceed
recommended_action: Check the system for lack of spare capacity and/or failed capacity, and either fix the problem or add capacity if necessary.

alert_message: Backward rebuild cannot proceed
recommended_action: Check the system for lack of spare capacity and/or failed capacity, and either fix the problem or add capacity if necessary.

alert_message: Rebalance cannot proceed
recommended_action: Add a physical disk; if this is not possible, reduce the spare policy while maintaining enough spare to sustain a rebuild, if necessary.

alert_message: The MDM cluster is degraded, and data is not protected
recommended_action: Check that all MDM cluster nodes are functioning correctly, and fix and replace faulty nodes, if necessary, in order to return to full protection.

alert_message: Cannot connect to the MDM cluster, but the cluster itself is operational
recommended_action: Check the connection to the MDM

alert_message: The MDM is not operating in Clustered mode
recommended_action: The MDM cluster was manually set to SINGLE mode. Working in SINGLE mode is not recommended. Single mode means that there is only one copy of the MDM repository. If you lose this copy, all System configurations and all the data on all the existing volumes will be lost. Please verify that this is an expected operation. Prepare the cluster modules (if needed), and return to CLUSTERED mode as soon as possible.

alert_message: Inactive Protection Domain
recommended_action: Protection Domain was inactivated by a user command. Confirm that this is an expected operation. This is usually done for maintenance. When maintenance is complete, reactivate the Protection Domain.

alert_message: Storage Pool has failed capacity
recommended_action: For the given Storage Pool, for some blocks, both primary and secondary copies are inaccessible. Check and fix the state of all devices in the Storage Pool and all the server's holding devices in the Storage Pool.

alert_message: Storage Pool has degraded capacity
recommended_action: For the given Storage Pool, for some blocks, one of the two copies is inaccessble. Check if a server is offline or if there is another server hardware-related issue. Check if a storage device is down.

alert_message: Capacity utilization above critical threshold
recommended_action: The capacity utilization of the Storage Pool is reaching a critical threshold. Remove unneeded volumes and snapshots, if possible, or add physical storage.

alert_message: Capacity utilization above high threshold
recommended_action: The capacity utilization of the Storage Pool is reaching a high threshold. Remove unneeded volumes and snapshots, if possible, or add physical storage.

alert_message: Failure recovery capacity is below the threshold
recommended_action: The capacity available for recovery in a degraded storage event is lower than the predefined threshold. Replace failed hardware or add more physical storage.

alert_message: Background device scanning has found data conflicts
recommended_action: Primary and secondary data copies do not match. Check the volumes running on the Storage Pool for any consistency issues. Contact Customer Support for assistance.

alert_message: Configured spare capacity is smaller than the size of the largest Fault Set
recommended_action: Increase the "spare percentage", configured in the Storage Pool and reserved for failure recovery, so that it is larger than the largest Fault Unit in the Storage Pool.

alert_message: The Storage Pool relies too heavily (over 50%) on capacity from a single SDS or Fault Set. Balance capacity over other SDSs or Fault Sets.
recommended_action: Move some physical disks from the large SDS to the others, or add disks to the smaller SDS in order to approximate the capacity of the large SDS as much as possible.

alert_message: Storage Pool does not meet the minimum requirement of 3 fault units
recommended_action: Add more SDSs to the Storage Pool to meet the minimum requirement of 3 hosts.

alert_message: There are cluster certificates pending approval. For more information, open System Settings > Connection.
recommended_action: Pending for approval certificates can be viewed and approved via System Settings > Connection.

alert_message: Master MDM Certificate is about to expire
recommended_action: Install a valid SSL certificate on the MDM before the old one expires

alert_message: Master MDM Certificate has expired
recommended_action: Install a valid SSL certificate on the host

alert_message: Secure connection disabled on MDM
recommended_action: Enable secure connections on the MDM in order to protect your login information

alert_message: The self-signed certificate presented by the Master MDM is not trusted
recommended_action: Check the certificate, and trust it if you see fit to do so

alert_message: MDM does not support secure connections
recommended_action: Check MDM cluster nodes

alert_message: The validity period of the certificate presented by the Master MDM starts in the future
recommended_action: The time and date on the computer where the certificate was created is not consistent with the time and date set in the PowerFlex OS system. Replace the certificate or fix the system time.

alert_message: The Certificate Authority that signed the Master MDM's certificate is not trusted
recommended_action: Trust the CA certificate if you see fit

alert_message: SDS is disconnected
recommended_action: The SDS service may be down or unreachable over the network. Verify that the SDS service is up and running and that the network is properly connected.

alert_message: SDS disconnects frequently
severity: 3 (Error) 2 (Warning) according to disconnect count and hard-coded
recommended_action: The SDS connection is fluctuating due to an unstable network connection. Check the SDS data network connection for Packet Drops, and try to disconnect one of the ports to see if the SDS disconnection issue is resolved by using only one port. If this does not resolve the issue, switch to the other port. If there is still an issue, it may be due to a Faulty NIC, Faulty Switch ports, or a faulty switch. If there is no issue with another switch, the issue was switch-related. Otherwise, the issue may be due to a faulty NIC, which requires NIC replacement.

alert_message: Memory allocation for RAM Read Cache failed on SDS
recommended_action: The system failed to allocate memory to the SDS RAM Read-Cache. For 32 GB RAM or less, up to 50% of the memory can be allocated for caching. From 32 GB or more, up to 75% of the memory can be allocated for caching. Reduce the configured RAM Read-Cache memory to match the allocation conditions.

alert_message: DRL mode: Hardened
recommended_action: DRL Mode is configured to "Hardened" instead of "Volatile". Both modes are configurable.

alert_message: RFcache card I/O error
recommended_action: Disable caching on the device and check the health of the device, because it may be faulty. If necessary, replace the device.

alert_message: RFcache skipped due to heavy load
recommended_action: Read Flash Cache is working under a heavy load, and therefore has skipped some IOs. This is a temporary error which should resolve itself. If it persists, try to balance the Storage Pool contents across more SDSs, or add more cache cards.

alert_message: RFcache IO stack error
recommended_action: IO has become stuck on the cache device. Disable caching on the device and check the health of the device, because it may be faulty. If necessary, replace the device.

alert_message: RFcache resources are low
recommended_action: There is not enough RAM available on the server for Read Flash Cache optimal operation. Increase the amount of available RAM.

alert_message: RFcache source configuration is not consistent
recommended_action: Check RFcache state of all disks in the pool and adjust them so that all disks have the same caching state.

alert_message: RFcache cache configuration is not consistent
recommended_action: Query the system to determine what is not consistent in the configurations of the Read Flash cache driver and the SDS where the cache device is located.

alert_message: RFcache device does not exist
recommended_action: You tried to add a cache device that does not exist. Check and fix Read Flash Cache configuration.

alert_message: RFcache API mismatch
recommended_action: The Read Flash Cache (xcache) driver version and SDS version do not match. Try to upgrade them to the same version. If the problem persists, contact Customer Support.

alert_message: SDS is in Maintenance Mode
recommended_action: The SDS is currently in Maintenance Mode. Exit Maintenance Mode once maintenance is complete. If an NDU is in progress, ignore this warning.

alert_message: Device failed
recommended_action: The SDS device could not be opened, read from or written to. Validate the device state. Check the cause of the error, and determine if it is a human error or a system malfunction. Check hardware if needed.

alert_message: Device test is done and device is pending activation
recommended_action: The SDS device has been added and tested. Activate the SDS device.

alert_message: Device has fixed read errors
recommended_action: Read from the SDS device failed. Data was corrected from the other copy. No action is required, but note that the device might be faulty.

alert_message: Device has fixed read errors
recommended_action: SDS device read failed more than 5 times. Replace the physical device.

alert_message: Device failed: All IO to the device will be stopped, and data will be relocated to another device.
recommended_action: Check the device, and if necessary, replace it

alert_message: Device failed: All IO to the device will be stopped, and data will be relocated to another device.
recommended_action: Check the device, and if necessary, replace it

alert_message: Device malfunction has been detected.
recommended_action: Check the device, and if necessary, replace it

alert_message: Minor failures have been detected in device performance.
recommended_action: Check the device, and if necessary, replace it

alert_message: Disk temperature is above the configured threshold, and may fail soon if no action is taken.
recommended_action: Check the temperature in the server and at the data center. Check if a fan alert is raised in the node.

alert_message: The disk is near the end of its working life, and should be replaced.
recommended_action: Replace the disk.

alert_message: The disk may be about to fail, or may be operating with reduced performance.
recommended_action: Consider replacing the disk.

alert_message: The SDC is either down or unreachable over the network
recommended_action: Verify that the SDC service is up and running and that the network is properly configured and connected.

alert_message: No more SDCs can be defined on this system; the maximum has been reached
recommended_action: The maximum number of SDCs in the system has been reached (1024^2).

alert_message: Errors have been detected on the Physical Disk
recommended_action: Replace the Physical Disk.

alert_message: Physical Disk state is erroneous (Hot Spare)
recommended_action: Errors have been detected on a spare Physical Disk. Replace the Physical Disk.

alert_message: The Physical Disk is not being used by the system
recommended_action: Access the storage controller and configure the disk.

alert_message: SSD lifespan is over endurance threshold
recommended_action: Replace the SSD device.

alert_message: The Physical Disk temperature has exceeded the configured threshold
recommended_action: Ensure that the server and its environment are properly cooled. Ensure that the internal chassis fans are working, and that there is adequate airflow.

alert_message: The Physical Drive's cache is not disabled
recommended_action: Disable the Physical Drive physical cache. Note: This may cause DI issues in power cycle scenarios.

alert_message: Device was requested to use as DAS Cache but it is not
The Logical Disk read policy is not set to "Read-Ahead"
recommended_action: Access the storage controller and change the Logical Disk's read policy to "Read Ahead".

alert_message: The Logical Disk IO policy is not Configured properly (Write-Back)
recommended_action: Check the Raid controller battery status, a fault in the battery will impact the Write-Back functionality.

alert_message: Logical Disk access mode is not set to "Read-Write"
recommended_action: Access the storage controller and change the logical disk's access mode to "Read-Write".

alert_message: The Logical Disk RAID level is not set to RAID0
recommended_action: The RAID type is set in the storage controller is incorrect. Access the storage controller, verify that the Logical Disk does not contain any data that is not backed-up, destroy the Logical Disk, and re-create the logical disk as RAID0.

alert_message: Logical Disk caching policy is not set to "DirectIO"
recommended_action: Access the storage controller, and change the RAID0 caching policy for the Logical Disk to "DirectIO".

alert_message: Logical disk is no longer being cached
recommended_action: Disable the Physical Drive physical cache. Note: This may cause DI issues in power cycle scenarios.

alert_message: The state of the backup battery in the storage controller is not optimal
recommended_action: The backup battery is not fully charged, but it will recharge itself while the storage controller is powered on.

alert_message: The backup battery in the storage controller needs to be replaced
recommended_action: The backup battery in the storage controller may be nearing the end of its working life. Replace the battery.

alert_message: The storage controller battery temperature is not within the configured threshold
recommended_action: Check the temperature in the server and at the data center. Check if a fan alert is raised in the node. Check the battery and the RAID controller and replace faulty items.

alert_message: There is no backup battery installed in the storage controller
recommended_action: Install a backup battery in the storage controller.

alert_message: The storage controller contains an incompatible battery pack
recommended_action: Replace the storage controller battery with one that is compatible with your controller's model.

alert_message: RAID controller's battery has invalid voltage state
recommended_action: Check the RAID controller's battery. It may need to be replaced.

alert_message: Hypervisor boot drive state is not OK
recommended_action: The drive on which the ESXi hypervisor is installed has reported an error. Replace the drive and reinstall the ESXi on the new drive.

alert_message: The storage controller is not operating optimally
recommended_action: The storage controller may be faulty, and should be replaced.

alert_message: The storage controller temperature has exceeded the configured threshold
recommended_action: Ensure that the server and its environment are properly cooled and that all the chassis fans are functional.

alert_message: CacheCade license is not installed
recommended_action: Install a CacheCade license on the storage controller.

alert_message: Some of the disk slots in the storage controller are empty
recommended_action: Add more disks if needed. If a disk was removed during a FRU (Field Replacement Unit) operation, insert the new disk into the chassis.

alert_message: The BOSS storage controller is in an Error state
recommended_action: The BOSS storage controller is faulty, and should be replaced.

alert_message: The BOSS storage controller is in a Degraded state
recommended_action: The BOSS storage controller may be faulty, and should be replaced.

alert_message: The physical CPU socket is not enabled
recommended_action: Enable the CPU socket in the server BIOS.

alert_message: Not all CPU cores are enabled
recommended_action: Change the physical CPU core configuration in the server BIOS.

alert_message: CPU is not operating at full speed
recommended_action: Set the CPU clock speed in the server's BIOS to its maximum speed.

alert_message: CPU temperature is not within threshold
recommended_action: The server's CPU temperature has exceeded the configured threshold. Make sure that the server is properly cooled and that the CPU and internal chassis fans are active.

alert_message: The CPU Voltage Regulator's temperature is higher than the configured threshold
recommended_action: Make sure that the server is properly cooled and that the CPU and internal chassis fans are active.

alert_message: The CPU Voltage Regulator's voltage is not within the configured threshold
recommended_action: 1. Verify that the power supply is functioning correctly. 2. Try to replace a port in the Power Distribution Unit, or supply an external power source to check it. 3. Replace the power cable. 4. Replace the Power Supply Unit module.

alert_message: CPU has invalid voltage state state
recommended_action: Check the chassis power supply

alert_message: RAM temperature is not within threshold
recommended_action: The temperature of one or more Server RAM modules exceeds the configured threshold. Ensure that the server and its environment are properly cooled and that all the chassis fans are functional.

alert_message: RAM voltage regulator's temperature is not within threshold
recommended_action: Ensure that the server and its environment are properly cooled and that all the chassis fans are functional.

alert_message: RAM voltage regulator's voltage is not within threshold
recommended_action: Ensure that the server and its environment are properly cooled and that all the chassis fans are functional. If the system still issues an alert, the DIMM may be faulty, and may have to be replaced.

alert_message: The node temperature has exceeded the configured threshold
recommended_action: Ensure that the server and its environment are properly cooled and that all the chassis fans are functional.

alert_message: The voltage values on the node are not within the configured threshold
recommended_action: 1. Verify that the power supply is functioning correctly, and then try to replace a port in the Power Distribution Unit or check with external power. 2. Replace the power cable. 3. Replace the Power Supply Module.

alert_message: SDC is not installed on this node
recommended_action: Consider installing an SDC on this node, so that it can use PowerFlex OS volumes

alert_message: Unable to connect to monitoring agent (VCenter)
recommended_action: Ensure that the vCenter configuration is correct, and ensure that the node's Mgmt port is routable to the vCenter IP address

alert_message: RFcache pool state is "Not Started"
recommended_action: Check the Read Flash Cache pool state. The cache device might be misconfigured. In this case, remove the cache device from the cache pool and add it back again.

alert_message: CMOS battery state is invalid
recommended_action: Check the node's CMOS battery. It may need to be replaced.

alert_message: Fan is disabled or removed from chassis
recommended_action: The fan is disabled or missing. Check the server's chassis fans and make sure they are inserted and working properly.

alert_message: CPU cache is not enabled
recommended_action: Enable the CPU cache in the server BIOS.

alert_message: CPU cache size is not optimal
recommended_action: Check the CPU cache in the server BIOS, and set it to maximum size.

alert_message: Fan speed is not within threshold
recommended_action: Ensure that the server is properly cooled and that the chassis fans are functional.

alert_message: The power supply input wattage is not within the configured threshold
recommended_action: 1. Verify that the power supply is functioning correctly. 2. Try to replace a port in the Power Distribution Unit, or supply an external power source to check. 3. Replace the power cable. 4. Replace the Power Supply Unit module.

alert_message: Power supply is missing
recommended_action: Install a new Power Supply Unit, or if there is an existing PSU, verify that it is properly connected.

alert_message: Power supply power loss
recommended_action: Check the power outlet/ power feed to the server.

alert_message: Unable to connect to ESRS Gateway
recommended_action: Check the network's connectivity to the ESRS Gateway.

alert_message: The system is not registered to ESRS
recommended_action: The system is not registered to ESRS and will not send Alerts to EMC for monitoring. Contact your Support \ Sales representative to get an EMC ESRS support package.

alert_message: ESRS number of messages received has been reached. No more messages will be sent in the next 8 hours
recommended_action: ESRS has a limit of receiving up to 200 alerts per 8 hours. The limit has been reached, so no ESRS messages will be sent in the following 8 hours.

alert_message: Network Adapter Link is down
recommended_action: The network link is down in the network adapter. Verify that the network adapter is enabled in the operating system, and verify that the network cable is connected properly at both ends.

alert_message: Device media type mismatch: device %s is an %s device, but its media type has been configured as an %s device
recommended_action: Verify the device media type and re-set accordingly.

alert_message: The automatic log collection directory is full
recommended_action: Delete some files from the directory: (Linux) /opt/emc/scaleio/gateway/temp/scaleio-auto-collect-logs/ or (Windows) C:\Program Files\EMC\ScaleIO\Gateway\Temp\scaleio-auto-collect-logs\

alert_message: There is not enough disk space to run automatic log collection
recommended_action: Delete some files from your disk

alert_message: Auto Collect logs started working, while cluster contains 1 or more ESX ips, with no credentials configured
recommended_action: Configure ESX credentials in lockbox, or by using the IM option.

alert_message: One SDC is disconnected from one SDS
recommended_action: Check the network links between the affected SDS and SDC

alert_message: One SDC is disconnected from one SDS IP address
recommended_action: Check the network links between the affected SDC and SDS IP addresses.

alert_message: One SDC is disconnected from all SDSs
recommended_action: Check the network links between the affected SDC and all SDSs.

alert_message: All SDCs are disconnected from one SDS
recommended_action: Check the network links between all SDCs and the affected SDS.

alert_message: All SDCs are disconnected from one SDS IP address
recommended_action: Check the network links between all SDCs and the affected SDS IP address.

alert_message: All SDCs are disconnected from all SDSs
recommended_action: Check the network links between all SDCs and SDSs.

alert_message: Disconnected network links between SDCs and SDSs.
recommended_action: Check the network links between all SDCs and SDSs.

alert_message: This SDC has not been approved
recommended_action: Add the IP address for this SDC to the list of approved IP addresses.

alert_message: This SDC does not have an approved IP address
recommended_action: Update the list of approved IP addresses for this SDC.

alert_message: Policy has been paused
recommended_action: Policy has been paused. You can resume policy.

alert_message: Auto snapshot creation failed
Drive is ready to be removed
recommended_action: The drive is ready to be removed from the node.

alert_message: BOSS is not configured with expected Raid Level 1
recommended_action: Check the BOSS setup configuration

alert_message: Interleave must be disabled in BIOS when NVDIMM devices are present
recommended_action: Disable NVDIMM interleave in BIOS settings.

alert_message: Persistent Memory must be enabled in BIOS when NVDIMM devices are present
recommended_action: Enable Persistent Memory in BIOS settings

alert_message: NVDIMM battery state is low.
recommended_action: Check the NVDIMM battery status. A fault in the battery will impact the NVDIMM persistent memory. Persistent memory will not function if battery is faulty.

alert_message: Install or check GPU drivers
recommended_action: Check that the GPU drivers are properly installed on the node.

alert_message: Replace DIMM
recommended_action: Replace DIMM

alert_message: DIMM reports Degraded state
recommended_action: Replace DIMM as soon as possible

alert_message: SDC has an IP address that is not approved by the MDM
recommended_action: Add the IP address for this SDC to the list of approved IP addresses.

alert_message: The V-Tree migration is paused due to internal error
The V-Tree migration is paused as either the source or destination Protection Domain is inactive
recommended_action: Activate the Protection Domain to resume the migration

alert_message: The V-Tree migration is paused as either the source or destination Protection Domain has degraded capacity
recommended_action: Resolve the issue causing the degraded capacity to resume the migration

alert_message: The V-Tree migration is paused as there is not enough space in the destination Storage Pool
recommended_action: Add capacity or remove volume to resume the migration

alert_message: The V-Tree migration is paused as there is unavailable capacity in the source Storage Pool
recommended_action: Resolve the issue causing the unavailable capacity to resume the migration

alert_message: Multiple V-Tree migrations are paused due to internal error
Multiple V-Tree migrations are paused as either the source or destination Protection Domain is inactive
recommended_action: Activate the Protection Domain to resume the migration

alert_message: Multiple V-Tree migrations are paused as the Protection Domain has degraded capacity
recommended_action: Resolve the issue causing the degraded capacity to resume the migration

alert_message: Multiple V-Tree migrations are paused as there is not enough space in this destination Storage Pool
recommended_action: Add capacity or remove volume to resume the migration

alert_message: Multiple V-Tree migrations are paused as there is unavailable capacity in the this source Storage Pool
recommended_action: Resolve the issue causing the unavailable capacity to resume the migration

alert_message: The Storage Pool has reached a high threshold for device capacity utilization
recommended_action: Remove unneeded volumes and snapshots, if possible, or add devices to the Storage Pool

alert_message: The Storage Pool has reached a critical threshold for device capacity utilization
recommended_action: Remove unneeded volumes and snapshots, if possible, or add devices to the Storage Pool

alert_message: The Storage Pool physical capacity is full and cannot serve new write requests
recommended_action: Remove unneeded volumes and snapshots, if possible, or add devices to the Storage Pool

alert_message: The Storage Pool has reached a high threshold for user data capacity utilization
recommended_action: Remove unneeded volumes and snapshots, if possible, or add devices to the Storage Pool

alert_message: The Storage Pool has reached a critical threshold for user data capacity utilization
recommended_action: Remove unneeded volumes and snapshots, if possible, or add devices to the Storage Pool

alert_message: The Storage Pool user data capacity is full and cannot serve new write requests
recommended_action: Remove unneeded volumes and snapshots, if possible, or add devices to the Storage Pool

alert_message: The V-Tree address space allocated for this Storage Pool is almost full. You may not be able to create new volumes in this Storage Pool.
recommended_action: Consider adding capacity to the Storage Pool or removing some V-Trees from the Storage Pool

alert_message: The V-Tree address space allocated for this Storage Pool is full. You cannot create new volumes, or enlarge volumes capacity in this Storage Pool.
recommended_action: Consider adding capacity to the Storage Pool or removing some V-Trees from the Storage Pool

alert_message: The V-Tree address space allocated for this Storage Pool is almost full. You may not be able to create new volumes in this Storage Pool.
recommended_action: Consider removing some V-Trees from the Storage Pool

alert_message: The V-Tree address space allocated for this Storage Pool is full. You cannot create new volumes, or enlarge volumes capacity in this Storage Pool.
recommended_action: Consider removing some V-Trees from the Storage Pool

alert_message: The V-Tree address space allocated in the system is almost full. You may not be able to create new volumes in the system.
recommended_action: Consider removing some V-Trees from the system

alert_message: The V-Tree address space allocated for the system is full. You cannot create new volumes, or enlarge volume capacity in the system.
recommended_action: Consider removing some V-Trees from the system

alert_message: The Storage Pool contains one or more devices with unexpected media types
recommended_action: Update the Storage Pool media type or replace the mismatched devices

alert_message: The Storage Pool has external acceleration configured without DAS Cache enabled
recommended_action: Update the Storage Pool cache settings

alert_message: The Storage Pool has DAS Cache enabled without external acceleration defined
recommended_action: Update the Storage Pool cache settings

alert_message: The Protection Domain has unbalanced data path support
recommended_action: Verify consistent Data Path configurations for all Host SDSs that support this Protection Domain

alert_message: The network interface %s is down in the SVM
recommended_action: A network interface is down in the SVM. Verify that the network interface is configured and enabled in the SVM operating system.

alert_message: System needs SVM Replacement
recommended_action: System node VMs are of an outdated OS version. Perform SVM Replacement

alert_message: Node needs SVM Replacement
recommended_action: This node has not yet undergone SVM Replacement procedure

alert_message: Inconsistent Read Flash Cache configuration
recommended_action: One or more of the Storage Pool devices belong to an SDS that does not have Read Flash Cache devices. Make sure all SDSs with devices belonging to this Storage Pool have Read Flash Cache devices.

alert_message: System log collection is in progress
recommended_action: Monitor the log collection process. You should not start another one before this one is completed.

alert_message: An unrecoverable state has been reported on the device: All I/O to the device will be stopped, and the data will be relocated to another device. The SDS does not recognize the internal signature on the device.
recommended_action: An unrecoverable state has been reported on the device: All I/O to the device will be stopped, and the data will be relocated to another device. The SDS does not recognize the internal signature on the device.

alert_message: The V-Tree migration is paused because either the source or destination Protection Domain has at least one SDS in Maintenance Mode
recommended_action: Exit the relevant SDSs from Maintenance Mode

alert_message: Multiple V-Tree migrations are paused because either the source or destination Protection Domain has at least one SDS in Maintenance Mode
recommended_action: Exit the relevant SDSs from Maintenance Mode

alert_message: The V-Tree migration is paused because of a connectivity error
recommended_action: Fix connectivity issue

alert_message: Multiple V-Tree migrations are paused because because of connectivity error
recommended_action: Fix connectivity issue

alert_message: Media type mismatch: HDD media type is not supported in storage pools with fine granularity data layout
recommended_action: Media type mismatch: HDD media type is not supported in storage pools with fine granularity data layout

alert_message: Invalid SMTP configuration for email notification
recommended_action: Check SMTP configuration of email notifications

alert_message: SMTP authentication failed
recommended_action: Check SMTP credentials for email notifications

alert_message: Error on sending notification email
recommended_action: Check connectivity with SMTP server

alert_message: Your system has insufficient NVDIMM capacity on %s to support future version upgrades. Required NVDIMM capacity for the upgrade is %s MB. Contact your account manager for more information.
recommended_action: Increase the NVDIMM capacity or reduce the SDS FG capacity before you upgrade

alert_message: Not all VASA providers are healthy
recommended_action: Check that all VASA providers are up and running, and that there are no errors
